payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Upon returning, the party had a plan to lead them off of a illusionary cliff, that went pretty well. Ended up taking out a significant portion of the charging army at the time. Vax here, however, got stunned on the way across by the one mind flayer, nearly fell, however was saved by Tiberius at the last moment. Nearly lost their magic carpet, but it was retrieved.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Brains!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Upon returning, the party had a plan to lead them off of a illusionary cliff, that went pretty well. Ended up taking out a significant portion of the charging army at the time. Vax here, however, got stunned on the way across by the one mind flayer, nearly fell, however was saved by Tiberius at the last moment. Nearly lost their magic carpet, but it was retrieved.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Brains!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You get a better look as you approach this entity. You see what looks like a very tall, thin, gaunt-looking humanoid with a hood over the head as you approach. You get a better look at the facial features. Smooth, kind of dull, bluish-purple skin. These sunken, creepy, yellowish green eyes, and instead of where a mouth or nose would be, you see four slightly twitching tentacles. Barely obscuring what looks like a round lamprey-like toothed maw.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, you make your way back to the tent that was originally scouted out. The rest of the party's there waiting as Clarota is awkwardly sitting in the far end, just looking out as all of you slowly approach and enter the tent. Not speaking, just reactionary, and keeping a watch on all of you.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I turn to Clarota, I'm like, "Ooh, that's our friend, Pike. She's a good ally. Hello, Pike!"

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Hello everybody.

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: (gasp) Pike!

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Sometimes we hug as allies, Clarota.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Where have you been? ASHLEY Well, I had quite a time. When we were up on the tower, I noticed something, and I felt... I had to leave, because I started to have a vision.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Oh, those are fun.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I feel like I should talk to you guys about this later, just because--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's personal?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: What is your name? I'm so sorry. I'm Pike, it's very nice to meet you.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Proverbial statement, I like that. At which point Clarota's eyes narrow, and you can feel just the general mood and energy of the inside of the tent grows dark. It grows mildly tense. Suddenly you feel this presence, a voice once again, fill your mind as Clarota says, "Seven years since I discovered my curse. My form was struck with the brand of the Arcane. And these foul arts proved difficult to conceal. My brethren could sense my impurity, my deviation, and cast me out. I was severed from the Elder Brain. And chased on threat of death, should I return. I wandered these tunnels in squalor, feeding on what paltry filth I could ensnare, and fleeing from the ones I could not face alone. In my wandering, I discovered a duergar stronghold, with a magma pool, and it proved to be a decent source of sustenance for some time. Paying off the occasional straggler. Suddenly a great battle took place. It appeared something had sieged the stronghold. Reading the surface thoughts of the deep dwarves I encountered in the following weeks, I found a shift of power. Whatever had taken this hold had now demanded fealty of them. And they accepted when faced with annihilation. This entity that took the duergar by force, they called K'varn. I caught no sight of it. But the fear I could sense, from within these doors were so primal, so great, that I knew to keep a distance to be safe. But four months ago one of my estranged people, was captured by these ash-skins, and brought within the obsidian walls, for what I assumed was interrogation. Long after, the bulk of the Duergar people marched in packs through the long, deep tunnels that led to my people's colony and attacked. Strangely, before much bloodshed could occur, which I only assume was the next step in this onslaught, the fighting stopped. And the colony allowed the ash skins to waltz directly into the temple, unharmed, unchallenged, where our Elder Brain resides. It wasn't until after I discovered whoever this K'varn is, they themselves infiltrated the temple, and somehow took the Elder Brain under their own control. I watched as my people had their will robbed of them. And they took the will of all the others that lived and breathed in these tunnels. Now, a terrible army is forged of both factions. And they walk under the banner of this K'varn. Since this, I've fled back to the higher tunnels, and scrounged for materials so I could shield myself from the iron grip influence of the now enslaved Elder Brain. It took me weeks. But I'm confident I'm safe from its influence. For now."

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yes. Of course.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very well, she may stay."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Thank you very much, Clarota.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I take my hand and scratch the back of my head. (all laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I take Pike and I kind of pull her out of the tent really quick, and I'm like--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Girl talk.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay he's like a mind flayer, and we found him in a hole under a waterfall, but he's cool, but he wants vengeance.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Is that-- the thing is covering, so he can't be--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's like outcast, yeah. But he's gonna help us fight all of his tribe who hates us.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Clarota.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Do we know for sure he's helping us?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can you help us understand your kind? Your history? How you've come to be here and who this individual is that is fucking with your shit? (all laugh)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Proverbial statement, I like that. At which point Clarota's eyes narrow, and you can feel just the general mood and energy of the inside of the tent grows dark. It grows mildly tense. Suddenly you feel this presence, a voice once again, fill your mind as Clarota says, "Seven years since I discovered my curse. My form was struck with the brand of the Arcane. And these foul arts proved difficult to conceal. My brethren could sense my impurity, my deviation, and cast me out. I was severed from the Elder Brain. And chased on threat of death, should I return. I wandered these tunnels in squalor, feeding on what paltry filth I could ensnare, and fleeing from the ones I could not face alone. In my wandering, I discovered a duergar stronghold, with a magma pool, and it proved to be a decent source of sustenance for some time. Paying off the occasional straggler. Suddenly a great battle took place. It appeared something had sieged the stronghold. Reading the surface thoughts of the deep dwarves I encountered in the following weeks, I found a shift of power. Whatever had taken this hold had now demanded fealty of them. And they accepted when faced with annihilation. This entity that took the duergar by force, they called K'varn. I caught no sight of it. But the fear I could sense, from within these doors were so primal, so great, that I knew to keep a distance to be safe. But four months ago one of my estranged people, was captured by these ash-skins, and brought within the obsidian walls, for what I assumed was interrogation. Long after, the bulk of the Duergar people marched in packs through the long, deep tunnels that led to my people's colony and attacked. Strangely, before much bloodshed could occur, which I only assume was the next step in this onslaught, the fighting stopped. And the colony allowed the ash skins to waltz directly into the temple, unharmed, unchallenged, where our Elder Brain resides. It wasn't until after I discovered whoever this K'varn is, they themselves infiltrated the temple, and somehow took the Elder Brain under their own control. I watched as my people had their will robbed of them. And they took the will of all the others that lived and breathed in these tunnels. Now, a terrible army is forged of both factions. And they walk under the banner of this K'varn. Since this, I've fled back to the higher tunnels, and scrounged for materials so I could shield myself from the iron grip influence of the now enslaved Elder Brain. It took me weeks. But I'm confident I'm safe from its influence. For now."

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yes. Of course.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very well, she may stay."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Thank you very much, Clarota.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I take my hand and scratch the back of my head. (all laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I take Pike and I kind of pull her out of the tent really quick, and I'm like--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Girl talk.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay he's like a mind flayer, and we found him in a hole under a waterfall, but he's cool, but he wants vengeance.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Is that-- the thing is covering, so he can't be--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's like outcast, yeah. But he's gonna help us fight all of his tribe who hates us.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Clarota.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Do we know for sure he's helping us?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can you help us understand your kind? Your history? How you've come to be here and who this individual is that is fucking with your shit? (all laugh)

payload.raw_text: So Clarota closes his eyes, and kind of goes into a meditative stance. The rest of you, those who have spells to choose, go ahead and choose your spells accordingly for the next day.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns_last5.text: Before I retire, Clarota, you said you wanted to speak with me later. Anything in particular, or you wish to save it for after the battle?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "Perhaps after. I just am curious about the source of your Arcane power."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns_last5.text: Ooh, I'd be happy to discuss it. I could learn a great deal from you as well.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns_last5.text: Later, of course. I go to my books.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So Clarota closes his eyes, and kind of goes into a meditative stance. The rest of you, those who have spells to choose, go ahead and choose your spells accordingly for the next day.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I was just trying to make an entanglement arrow. It's a trapper's arrow.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You manage to work out what seems like a tanglefoot bag-type detonation, where if an arrow were fired, it would be a grapple check against the individual, probably give them penalties until they're able to break free of the grapple. So yeah, success on that one.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nice job, Doogie.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm gonna be Hawkeyeing Laura out at some point, I got a couple of them.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Before I retire, Clarota, you said you wanted to speak with me later. Anything in particular, or you wish to save it for after the battle?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Perhaps after. I just am curious about the source of your Arcane power."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, I'd be happy to discuss it. I could learn a great deal from you as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Later."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Later, of course. I go to my books.

payload.raw_text: So as you all have your evening's rest... Not very comfortable. Number one, you're in the middle of a tent of a goblinoid city. Second, just the general proximity of sleeping near an illithid, you just have a general sense of unsettling dreams, imagery that is disturbing. Pike, you have many flashbacks to the vision you had, and actually wake partway through the night in a cold sweat. As you wake up, you all kind of slowly come to, it's about four hours into the evening's rest. You feel a slight tremor, a familiar tremor that you felt once before in this cavern. (rumbling) You feel the ground begin to vibrate and shake.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Just doing my vocal warmups. ♫ Me me me me me me me me me ♫

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: ♫ Molto bene, molto bene, molto bene, molto bene ♫

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: The human torch was denied a bank loan. (all laugh)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So as you all have your evening's rest... Not very comfortable. Number one, you're in the middle of a tent of a goblinoid city. Second, just the general proximity of sleeping near an illithid, you just have a general sense of unsettling dreams, imagery that is disturbing. Pike, you have many flashbacks to the vision you had, and actually wake partway through the night in a cold sweat. As you wake up, you all kind of slowly come to, it's about four hours into the evening's rest. You feel a slight tremor, a familiar tremor that you felt once before in this cavern. (rumbling) You feel the ground begin to vibrate and shake.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Before I retire, Clarota, you said you wanted to speak with me later. Anything in particular, or you wish to save it for after the battle?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Perhaps after. I just am curious about the source of your Arcane power."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, I'd be happy to discuss it. I could learn a great deal from you as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Later."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Later, of course. I go to my books.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So Clarota closes his eyes, and kind of goes into a meditative stance. The rest of you, those who have spells to choose, go ahead and choose your spells accordingly for the next day.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Rub it in. I'm sharpening my ax.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Good. Good.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It is good.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Just doing my vocal warmups. ♫ Me me me me me me me me me ♫

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm meditating.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: ♫ Molto bene, molto bene, molto bene, molto bene ♫

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The human torch was denied a bank loan. (all laugh)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Takes about an hour. You manage to find, essentially, a few piles of rather disgusting, sticky, goblinoid refuse, that you imagine will probably stick and burn pretty well. In a pitch-type way, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I had the same dream, Scanlan!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wow!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah guys, just so you're aware, every 50 subscribers we get, we are giving away an awesome Geek & Sundry game package.

preceding_turns.speaker: ZAC
preceding_turns.text: Ethanmyhead just won one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, there you go, congratulations! We already got a winner. We'll be putting those out for every 50 subscribers we get tonight. We'll be choosing one to win that awesome award, our wonderful package, so keep that in mind.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So the plan is again, you and I, stealth. The rest, attack with light? What's first, what's second, what happens here?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, I need to take a moment to find some-- I'm gonna look around and see if I can find any pitch, anywhere in the goblin camp.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, go ahead and make a survival check. Actually, this would be investigation.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Investigation. I got the exact same number, 12.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The poison does not seem to take effect, the body resists it. It is still alive. But it's clutching the back of its neck, as it turns around going (sputters), you can see its blood spurting out of its mouth, and its eyes are now just burning with rage. All the rest of these duergar are now reaching and pulling their weapons out. Everyone roll initiative. (all yell)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Your head comes to an extremely bright glow, all of you averting your gaze.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: This is stealth, right? Stealth for me?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, you're invisible.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Attack, attack, kill him!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That definitely rolls to hit, you have surprise. Go ahead and roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's a critical, right? I'm assassinating this mother fucker.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's a critical, technically. Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're going for the general?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The illithid can control us all.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Should we go in?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: We don't know anything.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So, roll damage on this.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Roll my damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: We just heard a scream. Oh, we can act on that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And I rage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Stone skin. Light on his head.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Rage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Going into a rage.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Daylight on my shield.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Daylight on your shield.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm so happy to be here. (all laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I will say for the purposes of this battle, there is no Trinket here, because Trinket would not have fit across the way to get there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Didn't you give her an arrow?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes, and you have an entrapment arrow now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I get ready for that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, DM? 62 into the back of his head.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: How?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Critical hit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You take the blade, and as it pierces the back--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, it's a poison one by the way.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Damn!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It pierces the back of its skull, you feel the muscles in its neck tense in reaction to the blade, but the blade slips past. You can see a gout of purplish-black blood begin to spill from its neck, as it instinctually reaches up, and pulls the blade from the back of its head. What's the DC on the poison?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I have no idea.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It should be on the blade info.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Poison dagger.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Fuck. Ah!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I think it's oh, it's 15.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: This was a good plan.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So you hold on that. It is now this dude's turn, the general. He, unaffected by this, it seems, just angrily leaps on to the table, runs across, (groans), and jumps down towards you. As he does, you see his physical form seems to enlarge. His actual small dwarvish form begins to swell, his muscles tense. He's about the size of a normal person, if not a little bigger right now. He comes at you with this giant black, jagged metal war hammer. (groans) Comes down to swing at you, as he comes down, you see it burst into flame. There's fire now surrounding the head of this war hammer as it arcs toward you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As immediately it crumples to the ground, lifeless, pull the blade from the maw, mind flayer down. (all cheer)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah Vax!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He was next, but not anymore. As it falls to the ground, all the rest of the duergar immediately have a reaction. We'll get to their turn in a second. Vex, you're up. So you're on the roof, up here right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Gotta get inside first.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We have to run down in?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Run down, you're on a wooden roof surface, you heard before there's an entrance here, and an entrance there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Windows are swing-down-in-able.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Unless Grog can just tear through the roof.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What was your initiative?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's wood.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You can hold initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I guess I'll hold, I'll hold, to see what the hell's happening.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right, so Grog, you manage to land on your feet. Pike, you actually plummet off and fall off to the side, right there. You are currently prone, and from the fall, which you guys both dropped down, you managed to reduce the damage, but you from the fall, you take eight points of fall damage. You are now flat on your back, the wind knocked out of you for a minute.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Come on.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You bring your ax up, you bring it down on top as hard as you can, go ahead and roll ax damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is that a d12?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's a d12 plus your strength modifier. Plus two 'cause you're raging.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Do I get advantage on that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Seven.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Plus two 'cause you're raging.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Nine damage, okay. As you swing the ax down, slam, it hits the wood. With the sheer weight of you there, and most of you guys conglomerated at that point, the actual roof breaks open and splits, into a small space. You slide down in the process.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I jump on to Grog's foot. (all laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Go ahead, both of you make an acrobatics check.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I wouldn't have had to do it. All right, well, I'm in the building.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Acrobatics check?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pike? You have disadvantage 'cause you're in plate armor.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Six.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: That's correct, so. Or if they're willing to give up the information, I have all that written down. He needed 14, he rolled a 12, but, because he-- There's a plus two modifier to the skill check, being a bard, and that was what he needed, he just made it. Basic, basic--

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The best way I know that happened was, we had been playing for a while, and we all work in the industry. People began to hear about our voiceover D&D game, weirdly. Like I'd come into sessions with directors I've never worked with before, they'd be like, "Oh yeah, you're the guy that runs that D&D game." How did that happen? How is that my thing? I'm cool with it, like heck yeah, my little high school self is like, I'm okay with being known about that. But then over time eventually Geek & Sundry contacted us. Felicia had heard about it. They called me in for a meeting, and we spent a number of months trying to figure out what format we were gonna try, if we wanna do it like multi camera, filmed canned episodes, then eventually the Twitch possibility came up, and we were like no, this is a much better format for us. And that's kind of how it happened.

payload.raw_text: It's beautiful, I spent two weeks in Gothenberg, Sweden, on business years ago. It was gorgeous. Food was amazing. Nothing like walking down the street and seeing a giant gothic stone structure with an archway and a gargoyle perched, and you walk in and it's a 7-Eleven. It's pretty hilarious. All the architecture there is old, but it's been repurposed, it's great.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Bad news bears.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: They're super awesome. One of these days we're gonna fight one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: One of these days.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: They're a signature character of Dungeons & Dragons. They represent D&D.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What made the dwarves get bigger today in the battle?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's actually an ability of the duergar.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Viagra.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. They can summon through sheer force of will, and just the way they've been bred over time, they can increase their form for a short time to increase their damage, and be just a general asshole, so. It's an ability the duergar have.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Long lost son.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm gonna guess that none of us have been to Sweden?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I have.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: My dad was from Sweden.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's awesome. I've been to Gothenberg.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's not surprising.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I have not, but I would like to go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, who wouldn't want to go to Sweden?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Makes sense. Pale and blonde.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Have you been?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Never been.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: What you hear in the show is a combination of a gentlemen named Kevin Macleod, who has a website called Incompetech, which he produces really amazing, royalty-free musical tracks. I've used them for many web series for many years. He's very talented, and I contacted him, and asked him permission to use on the stream. He's like, "yeah, anything on Twitch, go for it." And the other half are a lot of soundscapes I use from a company called Platemail Games, which make great custom RPG soundscapes, and some tracks that are just creepy, and dwarf cityscapes, and dark forest night. It's a great, great resource. Those are my two big ones for this Twitch stream.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Probably the--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Scanlan as Burt Reynolds.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We'll take about five more minutes of questions. Five or ten more minutes of questions, then we gotta take off.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How long will you be doing this show? 30 years.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Considering we've been playing for two and a half years, and everyone's level nine, we can keep going for quite some time. Whether or not we continue past that, means either we do a new campaign, or we go into epic or mythic levels? We'll cross that bridge when we get to it. I mean, I'd love to keep playing for as long as people wanna play, but also people get busy, life changes, we'll see how it goes. I'm in for the long haul as much as everyone else is.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Matt, multiple people are asking what the music is that we're hearing on the show.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: One day.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Grog, have you even thrown a gnome as an attack.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The very first time we played, I picked up Sam and chucked him across a room. That was full of a trap.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fastball special.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And it worked. It was awesome.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What character type would I play if I ever got a chance to play? I would probably be a bard. I wanna make a bard that actually conducts the battle. Meaning, sits there and watches the fight, and he's conducting, like music happens when other people attack. Cymbals go off, and drum beats, and all of his abilities are tied to music, so he's actually conducting the battle like a symphony. That's what I wanna make.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: That's badass.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: For our big session, I'd spend as much time preparing as we did playing. I'll put it that way, I spend as much time preparing as we play. So for these sessions, I'm spending about three hours a week, preparing, what, three to four hours a week? For old sessions that was bigger.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I do. As a reminder in case I forget.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Jarvis. (all laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I have notes written next to every NPC, like an accent or dialect that I chose for them, a general texture. Kind of like what you'd see for breakdowns for a character you're auditioning for. I basically make that for each NPC to remind me of what I've done. And if it's a particularly weird voice that I don't know if I could replicate in the future, after the session I'll go and do a little audio file of me doing the voice so I can go back to it later.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You're a freak of nature, man.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Seriously.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Good question.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: There's a lot that goes into it. I think Matt, just to put it into perspective, for everyone in the chat room, I think Matt probably spends close to eight hours, at least, on each time before we play. Or I guess at least we used to.
